Utilizing qualitative insights acquired from three rural South Sudanese communities—featuring interviews and focus group sessions with 94 research participants, and over five hours of communal radio material—this investigation indicates that gender narratives perceived as traditional by the South Sudanese, throughout a post-conflict transition sequence, do not necessarily present barriers to the fulfillment of gender and development missions.

Considerable differences were detected between the states in the percent change in age-standardized death rate for road injuries from 1990 to 2017, spanning from a decrease of 38·2% (22·3 to 51·7) in Delhi to a growth of 17·0% (0·6 to 34·7) in Odisha.

Significant variations were recorded between the states in the percentage alteration in age-adjusted mortality rate for road injuries from 1990 to 2017, going from a fall of 38·2% (22·3 to 51·7) in Delhi to an escalation of 17·0% (0·6 to 34·7) in Odisha.
